sections.about: This metric captures treated or untreated discharge to rivers, streams or other surface-water bodies under BRSR Principle 6 Essential Indicator water discharge by destination. Lower discharge is generally better where the strategic objective is recycling and ZLD. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: JSW and Jindal report zero in FY2024/FY2025, while Tata discharges 12.00 million kL to surface water in FY2025, down from 13.85 million kL in FY2024. SAIL discharges 41.81 million kL in FY2025, up from 22.99 million kL in FY2024, and notes treatment as P/S/T in FY2025. SAIL’s ZLD programme is therefore not yet translating into low reported discharge at the aggregate level, though it is treating and recirculating significant wastewater volumes.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 3rd. JSW and Jindal are best at zero; percentage gap is not meaningful, and Tata’s absolute gap is 12.00 million kL. Tata’s FY2024-25 trend improves, but rank is unchanged because two peers report zero.
sections.targets: Tata’s BRSR identifies ZED from Works premises and reuse of treated effluent in steelmaking processes such as coke quenching, BF slag granulation, gas cleaning, slag quenching and dust suppression. SAIL’s ZLD goal and upcoming-unit ZLD concept are relevant.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Treatment level matters. SAIL’s FY2025 discharge is reported with P/S/T treatment, so environmental impact is not equivalent to untreated discharge.
